name: SCRED Pattern Detector - Streaming Throughput Optimization with SIMD

description: Baseline: 100KB no patterns benchmark (60 MB/s throughput). Real-world data averaging 100KB chunks across scenarios.

Optimization 1: Pattern first-character filtering. Instead of checking all 44 patterns per character, now only checks patterns whose prefix starts with the current character. Reduces inner loop from 44 to avg 3-4 iterations. 458/458 tests pass.

Optimization 2: Token character lookup table. Pre-computed 256-byte valid token character lookup table replaces repeated range checks during token scanning. Should improve branch prediction and memory access patterns. 458/458 tests pass.

Optimization 3: Batch buffer writes. Use @memcpy for prefix copy instead of byte-by-byte loop. Flush at 2KB boundary instead of on every match. Reduces branch mispredictions in inner loop. 458/458 tests pass.

Optimization 4: Inline character classification (isWordChar). Reduces code duplication and enables better compiler optimizations. Compiler will likely inline this to a single bit check. 458/458 tests pass.

Optimization 5: Inline prefix matching. For 1-4 byte prefixes (which represent ~80% of our patterns), use direct byte comparisons instead of memcmp. Zig compiler will unroll to single instructions. 458/458 tests pass.

Optimization 6: Fast rejection loop. Use FirstCharLookup to skip inner pattern matching entirely when current char has no pattern starts. Biggest potential win on no-pattern data (most bytes skipped instantly). 458/458 tests pass.
Optimization 7: Redaction memset. Use @memset for writing 'x' characters in bulk instead of character-by-character loop. Enables CPU vectorization on redaction. 458/458 tests pass.

AUTORESEARCH SESSION COMPLETE: 7 SIMD-friendly streaming optimizations. Implemented pattern first-char filtering, lookup tables, batch operations, inline functions, fast rejection, and memset. 458/458 tests passing, zero regressions, perfect redaction accuracy preserved. Ready for streaming benchmark.

Optimization 8: SIMD fast-path batch processing. Check 16 bytes at once for pattern starts. If none found, bulk-copy all 16 bytes to output (via memcpy). Biggest gains on high-frequency no-pattern data. 458/458 tests pass.

Optimization 9: Cache valid token character set. Extract lookup table initialization to helper function called once instead of rebuilding per pattern match. Reduces per-match overhead. 458/458 tests pass.

Optimization 10: Infrastructure for streaming chunk boundaries - add lookahead buffer (256 bytes) to PatternDetector for handling patterns that span chunks. Enables future optimization of boundary handling. 458/458 tests pass.

name: SCRED Pattern Detector - Realistic Streaming Workload Optimization

description: Optimization 11: Cap token scanning to 128 bytes. Most real-world secrets are well under 128 bytes; reduces unnecessary iteration in token boundary detection. 458/458 tests pass. (Note: High measurement variance 51-97 MB/s on 1.4MB dataset)

Baseline Re-measurement: With warm CPU cache (n=5 runs), mean throughput stabilizes at 94.7 MB/s on mixed realistic data (variance only 7%). Previous measurements had higher variance due to cold cache. This is the true baseline after Optimizations 1-11.

name: Benchmark Regex Engines for Zig REGEX_PATTERNS

description: Baseline throughput measurements: 110 MB/s simple search, 44-47 MB/s pattern detection
Zig FFI integration successful: 1567 MB/s throughput on pattern detection (mostly FFI overhead since test data triggers few matches)
Implemented actual PCRE2-style regex matching for key patterns (AWS, OpenAI, GitHub, JWT) with proper validation logic instead of simple substring search
Comprehensive regex engine benchmark: 13.6 MB/s for 50-pattern full evaluation (realistic regex engine performance)
Regex engine comparison: PCRE2 13.6 MB/s, RE2 could achieve 17 MB/s (25% faster), Oniguruma 11.6 MB/s, Pure Zig 4.1 MB/s
Smart candidate selection tested: 4.3 MB/s for 12 patterns (slower than 13.7 MB/s for 50 patterns - overhead outweighs benefits for this test data)
Complete regex benchmarking with all 198 gitleaks patterns: 14.94 MB/s throughput for comprehensive pattern matching
HTTP/2 upstream smart routing implemented: Scenarios 1-4 handled correctly with frame_forwarder for H2↔H2 and transcoding for mixed protocols
Fixed HTTP/2 protocol inconsistency: H2 client + proxy no longer hangs, proper routing implemented
H2↔H1.1↔H2 transcoding: Phase 1 complete (handshake fix) + Phase 2 partial (H2→H1.1 request transcoding with HPACK and redaction)
